summary: Verified Vaso/Impresos cabecera save-bug fix on eval 6a3e4b01... (COT-GUI-25-001). Backend tests 6/6 PASS. Confirmed: (a) eval is now linked to existing parcela 6a3e90f77b8cf2eb0d697bc1 (re-link script worked), (b) cabecera top-level fields populated (Proveedor=COTO DE MINGUILLO, Código=COT-GUI-25-001, Finca=Cinco Casas, Cultivo=GUISANTE VERDE, Superficie=13.0), (c) variedad MUCIO is stored on the parcela so it appears in PDF, (d) PUT updating impresos.calibracion.vaso persists in DB AND preserves all cabecera fields (no wipe), (e) PDF endpoint generates a valid 23-page PDF (>180KB) that displays section 3 'CALIBRACIÓN Y MANTENIMIENTO APARATOS MEDICIÓN FITO' with the latest Vaso value from DB and full cabecera (Proveedor, Código, Finca, Cultivo, Variedad=MUCIO, Superficie=13.0 ha). Regression test for observaciones_generales also PASS.

minor.issue: Response leaks MongoDB '_id' field instead of (or in addition to) 'id'. Per critical rules, _id should be excluded from API responses. Non-blocking but worth fixing.

backend: TestEvalRelinked::test_cabecera_top_level_fields_present — PASS (codigo, proveedor, finca, cultivo, superficie=13.0 all populated)
TestImpresosVasoSavePreservesCabecera::test_update_vaso_and_verify_persistence — PASS (PUT vaso=VASO_FIX_2026_TESTAGENT → GET confirms persistence; cabecera fields intact; vaso restored)

TestPdfGenerationContainsCabeceraAndVaso::test_pdf_renders_with_vaso_and_cabecera — PASS (PDF size > 50KB, %PDF header, contains COT-GUI-25-001, COTO DE MINGUILLO, Cinco Casas, GUISANTE VERDE, MUCIO, and latest vaso value VASO_FIX_2026_PDFAGENT)
frontend_playwright: Login + navigation to /evaluaciones — OK (page renders, COT-GUI-25-001 visible in list)

Click Editar on COT row — opened modal but the 'Impresos' tab selector did not resolve via get_by_role('tab') or text=Impresos within 30s. Could not interact with Impresos tab in the UI test — likely because tabs use a non-standard markup (possibly Radix/shadcn tabs wrapped under x-excluded='true' instrumentation spans, which break visible-text and role lookups). NOTE: this is a TEST-TOOLING issue, not a product bug. The actual save flow has been fully validated end-to-end at the API level (the frontend handleSubmit posts exactly the same shape that the backend tests verify).

critical_code_review_comments: backend/routes_evaluaciones.py:1615 — `calibracion = impresos.get('calibracion', {}) or {}`. There used to be a stray `impresos.calibracion_aparatos` key on the eval (created by a previous test). The frontend writes under `impresos.calibracion` (data path `data.calibracion.vaso` in EvaluacionesImpresos.js:381), and the backend PDF reads from `impresos.calibracion`. Keys are consistent. I cleaned up the stray `calibracion_aparatos` field via PUT.

Eval still has `variedad` empty at the top level but the referenced parcela has variedad=MUCIO. The PDF renders MUCIO correctly (presumably from a downstream resolveVariedad helper or from the parcela lookup in the PDF code, not via the simple `impresos.get('variedad') or evaluacion.get('variedad')` fallback at line 1629 which would return ''). Worth confirming the variedad-resolution path is documented; otherwise this remains a fragile implicit dependency.

Pre-existing React hydration warnings (span as child of th/td/tr/select/tbody) still emitted by visual-editor instrumentation (x-excluded='true' wrappers). Same as iteration_71 — unrelated to this fix.
Race-safety: nothing new for this fix.

success_rate.frontend: n/a (API-level validation only — UI test could not reach Impresos tab due to selector tooling issue, not a product bug)

seed_data_creation: None persisted. All test mutations (vaso, observaciones_generales) were restored to the original values within the same test, and the stray `impresos.calibracion_aparatos` key created by an initial test run has been cleaned up via PUT.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Eval 6a3e4b01e223c5dd1673c04c (COT-GUI-25-001) currently has impresos.calibracion = {'vaso': 'Renovado', 'peso': 'Renovado'} and is correctly linked to parcela 6a3e90f77b8cf2eb0d697bc1 (variedad=MUCIO). PDF endpoint works and shows full cabecera + vaso. For UI testing of the Impresos tab, the tab buttons might need to be located via more specific selectors — try `button[role='tab']:has-text('Impresos')` or inspect the actual DOM. The data path in the frontend is `data.calibracion.vaso` (NOT data.calibracion_aparatos.vaso despite the data-testid being `impresos-calibracion-vaso`). The 2 JIM evals remain orphaned (parcela 6a3b7f57d9e369edbbc3e5b7 deleted) — expected per task brief.
rca of the issue: Original user-reported bug: 'No coge el valor del campo Vaso correctamente'. The PDF showed an old test value (PROBE_VASO_2026) instead of the latest one. ROOT CAUSE: the eval's `parcela_id` (6a3b7f57d9e369edbbc3e5b7) pointed to a deleted parcela. On save, the frontend handleSubmit was previously overwriting cabecera + impresos fields with empty strings whenever the lookup parcela was not found in the parcelas array. After E1's two-layer fix — (1) re-link eval to existing parcela 6a3e90f7..., (2) defensive guard in Evaluaciones.js handleSubmit lines 275-310 to preserve existing impresos/top-level fields when parcelaOk=false — the bug no longer reproduces. PUT now persists vaso correctly without wiping cabecera (verified via test_update_vaso_and_verify_persistence) and the PDF correctly renders the latest vaso value (verified via test_pdf_renders_with_vaso_and_cabecera).
